questionHtml: Explain how the two strands of the DNA double helix are held together.
markschemeHtml:
1. hydrogen bonding between nucleotides / bases;
  2. complementary base pairs;
  3. adenine-thymine and cytosine-guanine form base pairs (between the two strands with H-bonding);
  4. 2 bonds between A and T, while 3 bonds between C and G;

OWTTE

Full names required for c and d though use ecf.

meta.title: Explain how the two strands of the DNA double helix are held together.
breadcrumbLabels: A1.2.6. DNA as a double helix made of two antiparallel strands of nucleotides with two strands linked by hydrogen bonding between complementary base pairs
